question.text: Under Rule 3 of the PML (Maintenance of Records) Rules, 2005, every reporting entity shall maintain the record of all cash transactions of value more than:

answer.text: Rs. 5 lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency
Rs. 10 lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency
Rs. 15 lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency
Rs. 50 lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 3 of PML Rules requires every reporting entity to maintain records of all cash transactions of the value of more than Rs. 10 lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency. These are reported as Cash Transaction Reports (CTRs) to FIU-IND.
**The Trap:** Rs. 5 lakh is the threshold for cross-border wire transfer reports (CBWTRs), not CTRs. Rs. 50 lakh is the threshold for immovable property transactions (IPRs). Do not confuse these three thresholds.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is one of the most frequently tested numbers in NISM XXIV. It also appears in NISM III-A and III-C. Remember the threshold trinity: CTR = Rs. 10 lakh, CBWTR = Rs. 5 lakh, IPR = Rs. 50 lakh.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Also remember that series of cash transactions individually below Rs. 10 lakh but aggregating above Rs. 10 lakh within a month must also be reported. This prevents structuring/smurfing.
